Metadata.Case_Name: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd v Kao Chai-Chau Linda and another suit

Party_Details.Name: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd is a Singapore-incorporated company referred to in the proceedings as AT.
Peter Fong was the majority shareholder and Chairman of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd until his death on 25 April 2008.

From about 2006, a 51% shareholding in Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd was held by the Fong Foundation charitable trust, with the remaining shares held by Peter Fong, Linda Kao and various family members including Carolyn Fong.
Linda Kao Chai-Chau was appointed managing director of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd around 1996 by Peter Fong.
Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd engaged in business involving chemical-related transactions and business opportunities from Chinese chemical companies.
Carolyn Fong was a shareholder of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd and obtained leave to commence a derivative action on its behalf against Linda Kao.
Ernst & Young was appointed as Receivers and Managers of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd on 17 January 2012 pursuant to an application by Carolyn Fong.

Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, acting through Carolyn Fong, commenced Suit No 477 of 2012 as a derivative action against Linda Kao for alleged breaches of fiduciary duty in relation to certain transactions.

Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, acting through its Receivers and Managers, commenced Suit No 1015 of 2012 against Linda Kao and 15 other individuals alleging conspiracy to divert business away from the company and breach of fiduciary duty.
Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, through its plaintiffs in the two suits, objected to Linda Kao commencing third party proceedings against the Estate of Peter Fong.

Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, through its legal representatives in the two suits, filed written and oral submissions opposing Linda Kao's applications for third party notices seeking indemnity and contribution from the Estate of Peter Fong.
Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, through Carolyn Fong, later applied for leave to hand over conduct of the derivative action to the Receivers and Managers.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, acting through its derivative plaintiff and its Receivers and Managers, should be subjected to third party proceedings in its actions against Linda Kao by allowing Linda to pursue indemnity or contribution from the Estate of Peter Fong in respect of alleged diversions of business and breaches of fiduciary and director's duties.
Party_Details.Rule: Under Order 16 of the Rules of Court, a defendant may issue a third party notice to claim contribution or indemnity or to have related questions determined among the plaintiff, defendant and a non-party, and under sections 15 and 16 of the Civil Law Act a person liable for damage may seek contribution, potentially amounting to an indemnity, from another person liable for the same damage regardless of whether liability arises in tort, contract, breach of trust or otherwise.
Party_Details.Application: The court considered Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd's objections that the proposed third party proceedings against the Estate of Peter Fong were unnecessary, overlapped with Linda Kao's pleaded defences, and would cause additional expense and delay, and examined whether there remained factual and legal scenarios in which Linda could be found liable to Airtrust while still having a viable statutory contribution claim against the Estate of Peter Fong for the same alleged losses.
Party_Details.Conclusion: The appeals were allowed and Linda Kao was permitted to commence and amend third party proceedings against the Estate of Peter Fong in both actions brought by Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd.

Metadata.Case_Name: Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd v Kao Chai-Chau Linda and another suit

Party_Details.Name: Kao Chai-Chau Linda

Facts.Text: Linda was appointed as managing director of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd in 1996 by its then Chairman Peter Fong.
Linda remained the managing director of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd until 2012.

In Suit No 477 of 2012, Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd alleged that Linda breached her fiduciary duties in respect of certain transactions that mostly took place while Peter Fong was alive and Chairman.

In Suit No 1015 of 2012, the Receivers and Managers of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd alleged that Linda participated in diversions of business and funds to the detriment of the company after Peter Fong's death.
In Suit No 1015 of 2012, it was alleged that Linda and 15 other individuals were involved in a conspiracy to divert business away from Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd.
Linda's pleaded defence in both suits was that the impugned transactions and diversions were carried out pursuant to oral instructions or a standing understanding given by Peter Fong.

Linda asserted that the business opportunities alleged to have been diverted were not business opportunities of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd because she was receiving and disbursing funds on behalf of Peter Fong's friends and associates as a favour to them.
Linda applied in the derivative action for leave to serve a third party notice against the Estate of Peter Fong seeking an indemnity in respect of any liability she might incur.
In the RM action, Linda commenced third party proceedings against the Estate of Peter Fong seeking an indemnity in respect of any liability she might incur.
Linda applied to amend both third party notices to add alternative claims for contribution from the Estate of Peter Fong under section 15 of the Civil Law Act.

Linda argued that there was a possibility the trial court might find that she owed and breached independent fiduciary and director's duties to Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd even if she had acted on Peter Fong's instructions.
Linda contended that Peter Fong had given her instructions, including standing instructions or understandings, regarding how to handle the transactions and business opportunities in question.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Linda, as managing director of Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd, could pursue third party claims for indemnity or contribution against the Estate of Peter Fong in respect of alleged breaches of fiduciary and director's duties and alleged conspiracy to divert business and funds.
Party_Details.Rule: Under Order 16 rule 1 of the Rules of Court, a defendant may issue a third party notice to claim contribution or indemnity from a non-party in respect of the same subject matter, and under sections 15 and 16 of the Civil Law Act, any person liable in respect of damage suffered by another may seek contribution, which the court may apportion or convert into a complete indemnity, regardless of whether liability arises in tort, contract, breach of trust or otherwise.
Party_Details.Application: The court examined Linda's role as managing director, the allegations that she diverted business and funds and participated in a conspiracy, and her assertion that she acted on Peter Fong's instructions, to determine whether there was a realistic basis on which she might be found liable to Airtrust (Singapore) Pte Ltd while the Estate of Peter Fong could also be liable for the same damage, such that third party proceedings for contribution or indemnity should be allowed to proceed.
Party_Details.Conclusion: The appeals by Linda to commence and maintain third party proceedings against the Estate of Peter Fong were allowed, and her applications to amend the third party notices to include claims for contribution were also allowed.
